hypothesis: The systematic underestimation of band gaps by GFN1-xTB for MOFs containing 3d transition metals with open-shell configurations (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) can be corrected by incorporating a metal-specific DFT+U correction term as an additional low-fidelity baseline within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning hierarchy—where the first ∆ captures the GFN1-xTB to DFT+U correction and the second ∆ captures the DFT+U to HSE06 correction—such that this two-level scheme will achieve lower prediction error for transition-metal-containing MOFs than the single-level GFN1-xTB-to-HSE06 ∆-learning model, particularly reducing the mean absolute error below 0.20 eV for the subset of MOFs where the current model performs worst.
